title: icacorea aequilonga
intro: Icacorea aequilonga, also known as the "Gordolabio" or "Wild Potato", is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is a climber found in the tropical forests of Central and South America, particularly in countries such as Costa Rica and Panama. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Icacorea aequilonga include decoctions and infusions, which have been used to alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
